    36→        if type == .workoutCompleted {
    37→            if currentStreak >= 30 {
    38→                let bonus30 = XPTransaction(type: .streakBonus30, amount: XPTransaction.xpAmount(for: .streakBonus30), date: Date())
    39→                _ = try await xpRepository.awardXP(transaction: bonus30)
    40→                totalAwarded += bonus30.amount
    41→            } else if currentStreak >= 7 {
    42→                let bonus7 = XPTransaction(type: .streakBonus7, amount: XPTransaction.xpAmount(for: .streakBonus7), date: Date())
    43→                _ = try await xpRepository.awardXP(transaction: bonus7)
    44→                totalAwarded += bonus7.amount
    45→            }
    46→        }
    47→
    48→        let updatedXP = try await xpRepository.getUserXP()
    49→        let newLevel = updatedXP.level
    50→
    51→        return XPAwardResult(
    52→            previousLevel: previousLevel,
    53→            newLevel: newLevel,
    54→            xpAwarded: totalAwarded,
    55→            totalXP: updatedXP.totalXP,
    56→            didLevelUp: newLevel > previousLevel
    57→        )
    58→    }
    59→}

    10→import SwiftUI
    11→
    12→// MARK: - Error State Enum
    13→
    14→/// Tipos de estados de erro suportados
    15→enum ErrorState: Equatable {
    16→    case dependency(message: String)
    17→    case network(message: String)
    18→    case api(message: String, code: Int?)
    19→    case generic(title: String, message: String)
    20→
    21→    var title: String {
    22→        switch self {
    23→        case .dependency:
    24→            return "error.state.dependency.title".localized
    25→        case .network:
    26→            return "error.state.network.title".localized
    27→        case .api:
    28→            return "error.state.api.title".localized
    29→        case .generic(let title, _):
    30→            return title
    31→        }
    32→    }
    33→
    34→    var message: String {
    35→        switch self {
    36→        case .dependency(let msg),
    37→             .network(let msg):
    38→            return msg
    39→        case .api(let msg, let code):
    40→            if let code = code {
    41→                return msg + String(format: "error.state.api.code".localized, code)
    42→            }
    43→            return msg
    44→        case .generic(_, let msg):
    45→            return msg
    46→        }
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    var iconName: String {
    50→        switch self {
    51→        case .dependency:
    52→            return "exclamationmark.triangle.fill"
    53→        case .network:
    54→            return "wifi.exclamationmark"
    55→        case .api:
    56→            return "server.rack"
    57→        case .generic:
    58→            return "exclamationmark.circle.fill"
    59→        }
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    var iconColor: Color {
    63→        switch self {
    64→        case .dependency, .api:
    65→            return .red
    66→        case .network:
    67→            return .orange
    68→        case .generic:
    69→            return .red
    70→        }
    71→    }
    72→}
    73→
    74→// MARK: - Generic Error State View
    75→
    76→/// View genérica para exibir qualquer tipo de estado de erro

    78→    let state: ErrorState
    79→    var retryAction: (() -> Void)? = nil
    80→
    81→    var body: some View {

    90→

    92→                .font(.body)
    93→                .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textSecondary)
    94→                .multilineTextAlignment(.center)
    95→
    96→            if let retryAction = retryAction {
    97→                Button("common.retry".localized, action: retryAction)
    98→                    .fitPrimaryStyle()
    99→                    .padding(.top, FitTodaySpacing.sm)
   100→            }
   101→        }
   102→        .padding()
   103→    }
   104→}

Recebi uma recusa da apple, esse foi o review, The following information needs to be included within the app:

- A functional link to the Terms of Use (EULA)

You can use SubscriptionStoreView to easily include all of the required information in the app's purchase flow.

The following information needs to be included in the App Store metadata:

- A functional link to the Terms of Use (EULA). If you are using the standard Apple Terms of Use (EULA), include a link to the Terms of Use in the App Description. If you are using a custom EULA, add it in App Store Connect.

Next Steps

Update the app and App Store metadata to include the information specified above.

Resources

Apps offering auto-renewable subscriptions must include all of the following required information in the app itself:

- Title of auto-renewing subscription (this may be the same as the In-App Purchase product name)
- Length of subscription
- Price of subscription, and price per unit if appropriate
- Functional links to the privacy policy and Terms of Use (EULA)

     8→enum StoreKitProductID {
     9→    // MARK: - Pro Tier
    10→    static let proMonthly = "com.fittoday.pro.monthly"
    11→    static let proAnnual = "com.fittoday.pro.annual"
    12→
    13→    // MARK: - Elite Tier
    14→    static let eliteMonthly = "com.fittoday.elite.monthly"
    15→    static let eliteAnnual = "com.fittoday.elite.annual"
    16→
    17→    // MARK: - Sets
    18→    static let proProducts: Set<String> = [proMonthly, proAnnual]
    19→    static let eliteProducts: Set<String> = [eliteMonthly, eliteAnnual]
    20→    static let allProducts: Set<String> = proProducts.union(eliteProducts)
    21→
    22→    // MARK: - Tier Resolution
    23→    static func tier(for productID: String) -> SubscriptionTier {
    24→        if eliteProducts.contains(productID) { return .elite }
    25→        if proProducts.contains(productID) { return .pro }
    26→        return .free
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    static func isAnnual(_ productID: String) -> Bool {
    30→        productID == proAnnual || productID == eliteAnnual
    31→    }
    32→}

   582→
   583→                // Calculate streak
   584→                let calendar = Calendar.current
   585→                let today = calendar.startOfDay(for: Date())
   586→                let sortedDates = entries.map { calendar.startOfDay(for: $0.date) }.sorted(by: >)
   587→                if let mostRecent = sortedDates.first,
   588→                   let yesterday = calendar.date(byAdding: .day, value: -1, to: today),
   589→                   mostRecent >= yesterday {
   590→                    var streak = 1
   591→                    var currentDate = mostRecent
   592→                    for date in sortedDates.dropFirst() {
   593→                        let expected = calendar.date(byAdding: .day, value: -1, to: currentDate)!
   594→                        if date == expected {
   595→                            streak += 1
   596→                            currentDate = date
   597→                        } else if date == currentDate {

   601→                        }
   602→                    }
   603→                    streakDays = streak

# Fix: Add Terms of Use & Privacy Policy links to Paywall (App Store Rejection)

## Context

Apple rejected the app because the subscription paywall (`OptimizedPaywallView.swift`) does not include functional links to Terms of Use (EULA) and Privacy Policy. Apple requires all auto-renewable subscription flows to include:
- Title of auto-renewing subscription
- Length of subscription
- Price of subscription
- **Functional links to Privacy Policy and Terms of Use (EULA)**

The paywall currently only has a text disclaimer ("A assinatura é renovada automaticamente...") in the `legalFooter` section (line 300-308) with **no clickable links**. The links already exist in `LegalSection.swift` and `AppInfoFooter.swift` pointing to `https://fittoday.app/terms` and `https://fittoday.app/privacy`.

## Plan

### 1. Add Terms & Privacy links to the paywall's `legalFooter`

**File:** `FitToday/Presentation/Features/Pro/OptimizedPaywallView.swift` (lines 300-308)

Update the `legalFooter` computed property to add the Terms and Privacy links below the existing auto-renewal disclaimer text. Reuse the same URLs already used in `AppInfoFooter.swift`:

```swift
private var legalFooter: some View {
    VStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.xs) {
        Text("paywall.legal.auto_renew".localized)
            .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 11, weight: .medium))
            .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textTertiary)
            .multilineTextAlignment(.center)

        HStack(spacing: FitTodaySpacing.md) {
            Link("paywall.legal.terms".localized, destination: URL(string: "https://fittoday.app/terms")!)
            Text("•").foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.textTertiary)
            Link("paywall.legal.privacy".localized, destination: URL(string: "https://fittoday.app/privacy")!)
        }
        .font(FitTodayFont.ui(size: 11, weight: .medium))
        .foregroundStyle(FitTodayColor.brandPrimary)
    }
    .padding(.horizontal, FitTodaySpacing.sm)
}
```

### 2. Add localization keys

**Files:**
- `Resources/en.lproj/Localizable.strings`
- `Resources/pt-BR.lproj/Localizable.strings`

Add keys:
- `"paywall.legal.auto_renew"` — existing disclaimer text
- `"paywall.legal.terms"` — "Terms of Use" / "Termos de Uso"
- `"paywall.legal.privacy"` — "Privacy Policy" / "Política de Privacidade"

### 3. Commit

## Files to Modify

1. `FitToday/FitToday/Presentation/Features/Pro/OptimizedPaywallView.swift` — add links to `legalFooter`
2. `FitToday/FitToday/Resources/en.lproj/Localizable.strings` — add 3 keys
3. `FitToday/FitToday/Resources/pt-BR.lproj/Localizable.strings` — add 3 keys

## Verification

1. Build the project
2. Open the paywall screen and confirm Terms/Privacy links appear below the auto-renewal text
3. Tap each link to verify they open in Safari
